arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; dpkg -l | grep -i llvm 2>/dev/null | head -5

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
# Try downloading debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server
# BuildId: 1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"

# Try different URL formats
echo "=== Try 1: ELF buildid sym format ==="
curl -sSL -w "%{http_code}" -o /dev/null "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug"

echo ""
echo "=== Try 2: Direct library with buildid ==="
curl -sSL -w "%{http_code}" -o /dev/null "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so"

echo ""
echo "=== Try 3: debuginfod ==="
curl -sSL -w "%{http_code}" -o /dev/null "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so"
arguments.description: Try downloading symbols from MS symbol server

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Try downloading symbols from MS symbol server

arguments.command:
# Also try debuginfod protocol
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
echo "=== debuginfod.elfutils.org ==="
curl -sSL -w "%{http_code}" -o /dev/null "https://debuginfod.elfutils.org/buildid/${BUILDID}/debuginfo"
echo ""
echo "=== Microsoft debuginfod ==="
curl -sSL -w "%{http_code}" -o /dev/null "https://symbolserver.unity3d.com/buildid/${BUILDID}/debuginfo" 2>/dev/null
echo ""
# Try NuGet-based approach - check if we can identify the runtime version
echo "=== Try to identify .NET version from BuildId ==="
curl -sSL -w "\n%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/${BUILDID}ffffffff/libmonosgen-2.0.so" -o /dev/null 2>&1

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to symbolicate the .NET runtime frames in this tombstone. The key information is:

1. The library is `libmonosgen-2.0.so` with BuildId `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`
2. The frames are at offsets: `0x222098`, `0x2241ac`, `0x1abc80`, `0x1abd14`, `0x16dfa4`, `0x16db6c`, `0x16de04`

To symbolicate these, I would need the debug symbols for this specific build of `libmonosgen-2.0.so`. 

Microsoft publishes symbol packages for .NET runtime libraries through their symbol server at `https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols`, where I can fetch the debug symbols using the BuildId to construct the proper path for the ELF binary.

I'm considering a few approaches: using the `dotnet-symbol` tool if it's available, trying the debuginfod protocol, or manually downloading from the Microsoft symbol server. Let me start by checking what tools I have access to and then attempt to retrieve the symbols.

Actually, I might not have the .NET SDK installed, so let me think about this more practically. I could download the debug symbols directly from Microsoft's symbol server using the ELF build ID format, then use tools like `addr2line` or `llvm-symbolizer` to resolve the addresses.

The build ID from the stack trace is `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`, so I'll try downloading the symbols and see if I can actually retrieve them—though this might be a test file with fabricated data anyway.

arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; dpkg -l | grep -i llvm 2>/dev/null | head -5

# Try different URL formats
echo "=== Try 1: ELF buildid sym format ==="
curl -sSL -w "%{http_code}" -o /dev/null "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug"

echo ""
echo "=== Try 2: Direct library with buildid ==="
curl -sSL -w "%{http_code}" -o /dev/null "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so"

echo ""
echo "=== Try 3: debuginfod ==="
curl -sSL -w "%{http_code}" -o /dev/null "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so"", "description": "Try downloading symbols from MS symbol server"}

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args:
{"command": "# Also try debuginfod protocol
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
echo "=== debuginfod.elfutils.org ==="
curl -sSL -w "%{http_code}" -o /dev/null "https://debuginfod.elfutils.org/buildid/${BUILDID}/debuginfo"
echo ""
echo "=== Microsoft debuginfod ==="
curl -sSL -w "%{http_code}" -o /dev/null "https://symbolserver.unity3d.com/buildid/${BUILDID}/debuginfo" 2>/dev/null
echo ""
# Try NuGet-based approach - check if we can identify the runtime version
echo "=== Try to identify .NET version from BuildId ==="
curl -sSL -w "\n%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/${BUILDID}ffffffff/libmonosgen-2.0.so" -o /dev/null 2>&1", "description": "Try debuginfod sources"}

toolArgs.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; dpkg -l | grep -i llvm 2>/dev/null | head -5
